metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen AC4 to require evidence of a *successful* workflow run (e.g., link to a green Actions run URL and the Security tab showing at least one completed scan), and/or add a note distinguishing a zero-finding success from a run error.
findings.finding: AC4 (post-landing triage comment) is post-sprint-only validation that cannot be evaluated in-session; it also does not distinguish a successful workflow run from a failed/errored one — a workflow that errors would produce no findings to triage yet AC4 could still be 'satisfied' (with a comment saying '0 findings because the run errored').
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4 '(post-landing, tracked) after the stack lands, a comment records the first CodeQL run's finding count and its triage disposition'
findings.suggested_fix: Revise AC4 to: '(post-landing) a comment links to the first successful CodeQL Actions run (green checkmark, non-errored), records the finding count, and records triage disposition; if the run errors, a follow-up sub-task is opened to fix the workflow before closing.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add `CONTRIBUTING.md` to the in-scope file list (updating line 389 to match the plan's actual triggers — drop 'on mirror PRs'), or add a `pull_request` trigger to the workflow to match the existing CONTRIBUTING.md description; in both cases enumerate CONTRIBUTING.md as a co-edited file.
findings.finding: `CONTRIBUTING.md` already references `.github/workflows/codeql.yml` and describes it as running on 'mirror PRs' (a `pull_request` trigger) in addition to push-to-main and weekly, but the plan specifies only `push` to `main` + weekly `schedule` triggers. This makes CONTRIBUTING.md an unenumerated consumer whose stated description will be factually inconsistent with the workflow the plan creates.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `CONTRIBUTING.md` to the 'In:' scope line and update line 389 to read: 'It runs on push to `main` (the post-merge tree) and weekly.' to match the plan's push+schedule-only trigger set.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the repo contains committed JS/TS source files that CodeQL can analyze before including `javascript` in the language matrix; if no such files exist, drop the `javascript` leg (or add a `if: always()` + `continue-on-error` guard with an explanatory comment) to prevent a 'no code found' failure on every run.
findings.finding: The plan includes `javascript` in the CodeQL language matrix, but the repo appears to contain no committed JavaScript or TypeScript source files (src/rebar is pure Python; JS appears only in ephemeral test fixtures created at runtime in temp dirs). CodeQL requires committed JS/TS files to analyze; without them the `javascript` matrix leg may emit a 'no code found' error or fail the workflow run.
findings.suggested_fix: If JS/TS source files are absent: remove `javascript` from the matrix and note in a comment that the leg can be re-added when JS/TS source is introduced. If they are present (e.g., in a subdirectory not visible in the top-level listing), add a comment in the workflow citing their location so the rationale is grep-verifiable, matching the documentation standard of the other workflows.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the plan's assertion that 'no other workflow here uses SHA-pinning' — gerrit-verify.yaml uses SHA-pinned refs at lines 70, 129, and 135; explicitly decide and document whether `github/codeql-action@v3` should follow major-version-tag pinning (acceptable for GitHub-owned actions) or SHA-pinning, and update AC line 3 accordingly.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'No SHA-pinning (no other workflow here uses it)' as justification for using only major-version-tag pinning in the new CodeQL workflow — but gerrit-verify.yaml already uses SHA-pinned actions at lines 70, 129 (lfreleng-actions/checkout-gerrit-change-action@f87b90a3370e62dad310797fedc7fd3700c75832) and line 135 (actions/setup-python@ece7cb06caefa5fff74198d8649806c4678c61a1). The premise used to exclude SHA-pinning from the new workflow is false.
findings.location: What/files section — 'No SHA-pinning (no other workflow here uses it)'; AC line 3 — 'no bare @main/@sha in the new file'
findings.suggested_fix: Update the 'What/files' section to: 'Pin actions by MAJOR VERSION TAG consistent with the repo convention for GitHub-owned actions (`actions/*`). Note: gerrit-verify.yaml does SHA-pin third-party actions (lfreleng-actions/*); github/codeql-action is a GitHub-owned action and major-version-tag pinning (@v3) is consistent with how all actions/* actions are pinned across the repo.' Remove the false 'no other workflow here uses it' clause.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the intended cron schedule explicitly in the plan (e.g., `'0 3 * * 1'` — 03:00 UTC Monday) so the executor has a concrete default to act on.
findings.finding: The weekly `schedule` cron expression is described only as 'weekly' without specifying day or time; an executor must invent a value.
findings.location: What / files — 'Triggers: push to main + a weekly schedule cron'; AC2 — 'a weekly schedule trigger exists'
findings.suggested_fix: In 'What / files' and AC2, add the cron expression: `schedule: [{cron: '0 3 * * 1'}]` (or author's preferred time), removing ambiguity.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the expected CodeQL `build-mode` for the `javascript` matrix entry (e.g. `build-mode: none` or a note that autobuild's source-scan fallback is intentional), given the repo has no root-level `package.json` or JS build pipeline.
findings.finding: The plan specifies a `javascript` language matrix entry but does not describe how CodeQL should handle the absence of a JS build system. The repo has no root-level `package.json` or JS build pipeline; CodeQL's default `autobuild` step will emit a 'no build system detected' diagnostic and fall back to source-only scanning. An implementer unaware of this fallback may add an unnecessary build step, suppress the warning incorrectly, or misread the first scan's diagnostic output as a configuration error.
findings.suggested_fix: In the What/files section, add a note: 'For the `javascript` matrix entry, set `build-mode: none` (or rely on autobuild's source-scan fallback — no npm build pipeline exists); the extractor will scan `.js`/`.ts` source files directly without a build step.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the convention citation: gerrit-verify uses `lfreleng-actions/checkout-gerrit-change-action@SHA` (not `actions/checkout@v5`) and SHA-pins `setup-python`; cite only `test.yml` (and other workflows) as the basis for major-version-tag pinning of `actions/*` actions.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'test.yml/gerrit-verify use `actions/checkout@v5`' as the pinning convention basis, but gerrit-verify.yaml does NOT use `actions/checkout@v5` — it uses `lfreleng-actions/checkout-gerrit-change-action@SHA` (a different action entirely, SHA-pinned). The plan misidentifies the action used in gerrit-verify.
findings.location: What/files section — 'Pin actions by MAJOR VERSION TAG to match repo convention (test.yml/gerrit-verify use actions/checkout@v5, actions/setup-python@v6)'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
